'''Vocatara''' was an individual who served in the [[Republic Army|Army]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] as a [[Rocket-Jumper Elite Advance Unit|rocket-jumper]] sometime before the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[4800 BBY]]. By then, Vocatara led the Republic as a [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]], at which [[Time/Legends|time]] the [[Gank/Legends|Gank]] [[Species/Legends|species]] had begun [[Speciecide|exterminating]] other species in a conflict that came to be known as the [[Gank Massacres]]. In response to the conflict, Vocatara commissioned the creation of the [[juggernaut war droid]]s to assist Republic forces and the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] with the defeat of the Ganks.

==Biography==
As a [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Rocket-Jumper Elite Advance Unit|rocket-jumper]], the individual known as Vocatara served as one of the [[Jetpack/Legends|jetpack]]-equipped elite [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] of the [[Republic Army]].<ref name="GatW" /> Vocatara received many decorations while with the rocket-jumpers.<ref name="Blog">{{SW|url=news/the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-part-4-the-rise-of-the-republic-needs-author-entry|series=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|text=Part 4: The Rise of the Republic}}</ref> Like several other members of the rocket-jumpers, Vocatara went on to serve the Republic as a [[Politics/Legends|politician]].<ref name="GatW" /> By the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[4800 BBY]], Vocatara led the Republic as [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] of the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]]. At that [[Time/Legends|time]], while Vocatara was in [[Office of the Supreme Chancellor/Legends|office]],<ref name="Droids" /> a [[Species/Legends|species]] known as the [[Gank/Legends|Ganks]] had begun [[Speciecide|exterminating]] other species after they had been hired by the [[Neimoidian/Legends|Neimoidian]] species to deal with the [[Porporite]]s, a species that had gone into a [[Death/Legends|homicidal]] frenzy after becoming addicted to the [[Spice/Legends|drug]] known as [[Ryll/Legends|ryll]].<ref name="TOR">{{TORcite|type=codex|Galactic History 35: The Gank Massacres}}</ref>

In response to the Gank threat, which later became known as the [[Gank Massacres]], Chancellor Vocatara quickly ordered the commissioning of the first [[juggernaut war droid]]s<ref name="Droids" /> to assist Republic forces and the [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] peacekeepers known as the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] in their fight against the Ganks.<ref name="TCSWE1">{{CSWECite|III|309|Vocatara}}</ref> By the year [[4775 BBY]], the conflict had ended, but Vocatara's commissioning of the juggernaut war [[Droid/Legends|droids]] later harmed the Republic when the droids went rogue on the Republic's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] during the [[Great Droid Revolution]] in [[4015 BBY]].<ref name="GatW" />

==Personality and traits==
Before becoming a politician, Vocatara was skilled enough as a soldier to be a member of the elite Republic rocket-jumpers, which required knowledge in the use of jetpacks.<ref name="GatW" /> During the Gank Massacres, Vocatara was forced to commission the juggernaut war droids,<ref name="TOR" /> which he performed quickly in order provide aid to Republic forces.<ref name="TCSWE1" />

==Behind the scenes==
Vocatara was first mentioned in the juggernaut war droid entry in ''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'', a [[2006]] reference book written by [[Daniel Wallace]].<ref name="Droids" /> Chancellor Vocatara was later mentioned in ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'', a [[2011]] [[Star Wars video games|MMORPG]] produced by [[BioWare]], as part of a codex entry for a [[Datacron/Legends|datacron]] containing information about the Gank Massacres.<ref name="TOR" /> A section on the rocket-jumpers mentioning Vocatara was written by [[Jason Fry]] for ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'', a reference book released in [[2012]], although Fry ultimately cut the section from the book. In [[2014]], the section was released on the [[Star Wars Blog|''Star Wars'' Blog]] in the fourth part of a [[Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|series]] on cut content from ''The Essential Guide to Warfare'' written by Fry and [[Erich Schoeneweiss]].<ref name="Blog" />
